ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "area" REAL /* Total area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "currency_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the currency used in the country */,
  "capital_city_id" INTEGER /* ID of the capital city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_capital_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("capital_city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "territories" (
  "territory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each territory */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the territory belongs to */,
  "territory_name" TEXT /* Name of the territory */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the territory */,
  "area" REAL /* Total area of the territory in square kilometers */,
  "governor_name" TEXT /* Name of the governor or head of the territory */,
  "capital_city_id" INTEGER /* ID of the capital city of the territory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("territory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_territories_capital_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("capital_city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_territories_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "territory_id" INTEGER /* ID of the territory the city belongs to */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the city */,
  "area" REAL /* Total area of the city in square kilometers */,
  "mayor_name" TEXT /* Name of the mayor or head of the city */,
  "location_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the type of location */,
  "classification_id" INTEGER /* ID of the classification of the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cities_classification_id FOREIGN KEY ("classification_id") REFERENCES location_classifications ("classification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cities_territory_id FOREIGN KEY ("territory_id") REFERENCES territories ("territory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cities_location_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_type_id") REFERENCES location_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (nullable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the type */,
  "parent_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent type (nullable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_classifications" (
  "classification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each classification */,
  "classification_name" TEXT /* Name of the classification */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the classification */,
  "parent_classification_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent classification (nullable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("classification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location the tag is associated with */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  "tag_type" TEXT /* Type of the tag (e.g., demographic, economic) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_location_tags_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_hierarchies" (
  "hierarchy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hierarchy */,
  "parent_location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent location */,
  "child_location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the child location */,
  "hierarchy_level" INTEGER /* Level of the hierarchy (e.g., 1 for country, 2 for territory, 3 for city) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hierarchy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_location_hierarchies_child_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("child_location_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_location_hierarchies_parent_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_location_id") REFERENCES territories ("territory_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the location */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the location was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crm_data" (
  "crm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each CRM record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the customer */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location associated with the customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "customer_email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "customer_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "customer_address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "last_interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the last interaction with the customer */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of the last interaction (e.g., email, call) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crm_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crm_data_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
